core_mechanism: dependence-preserving resampling (block/residual bootstrap): approximating the sampling distribution of a statistic by repeatedly drawing surrogate datasets from an observed correlated sequence in contiguous blocks (or from fitted residuals) so that the resampling preserves the sequence's short-range correlation structure rather than assuming exchangeable/independent draws
brief_reason: The abstract's method rests on block, residual, sieve, and wild resampling to build distribution-free confidence intervals, motivated by the finding that the exchangeable (IID) bootstrap undercovers under dependence while dependence-aware block/sieve resampling restores near-nominal coverage.
